fails.q: Show the name and the release year of the song by the youngest singer.
fails.gold: SELECT song_name ,  song_release_year FROM singer ORDER BY age LIMIT 1
fails.pred:
SELECT s.Name, s.Song_release_year
FROM singer s
WHERE s.Age = (SELECT MIN(Age) FROM singer)

fails.q: What is the maximum capacity and the average of all stadiums ?
fails.gold: select max(capacity), average from stadium
fails.pred: SELECT MAX(Capacity) AS max_capacity, AVG(Capacity) AS average_capacity FROM stadium

fails.q: Show the stadium name and the number of concerts in each stadium.
fails.gold: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id  =  T2.stadium_id GROUP BY T1.stadium_id
fails.pred:
SELECT stadium.Name AS stadium_name, COUNT(concert.concert_ID) AS concert_count
FROM stadium
LEFT JOIN concert ON stadium.Stadium_ID = concert.Stadium_ID
GROUP BY stadium.Stadium_ID, stadium.Name

fails.q: For each stadium, how many concerts play there?
fails.gold: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM concert AS T1 JOIN stadium AS T2 ON T1.stadium_id  =  T2.stadium_id GROUP BY T1.stadium_id
fails.pred:
SELECT s.Stadium_ID, COUNT(c.concert_ID) AS concert_count
FROM stadium s
LEFT JOIN concert c ON s.Stadium_ID = c.Stadium_ID
GROUP BY s.Stadium_ID

fails.q: Show countries where a singer above age 40 and a singer below 30 are from.
fails.gold: SELECT country FROM singer WHERE age  >  40 INTERSECT SELECT country FROM singer WHERE age  <  30
fails.pred:
SELECT DISTINCT s.Country
FROM singer s
WHERE (s.Age > 40 OR s.Age < 30)

fails.q: List singer names and number of concerts for each singer.
fails.gold: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.singer_id  =  T2.singer_id GROUP BY T2.singer_id
fails.pred:
SELECT s.Name, COUNT(sc.concert_ID) AS concert_count
FROM singer s
LEFT JOIN singer_in_concert sc ON s.Singer_ID = sc.Singer_ID
GROUP BY s.Singer_ID, s.Name

fails.q: What are the names of the singers and number of concerts for each person?
fails.gold: SELECT T2.name ,  count(*) FROM singer_in_concert AS T1 JOIN singer AS T2 ON T1.singer_id  =  T2.singer_id GROUP BY T2.singer_id
fails.pred:
SELECT s.Name, COUNT(sic.concert_ID) AS concert_count
FROM singer s
LEFT JOIN singer_in_concert sic ON s.Singer_ID = sic.Singer_ID
GROUP BY s.Singer_ID, s.Name

fails.q: Find the maximum weight for each type of pet. List the maximum weight and pet type.
fails.gold: SELECT max(weight) ,  petType FROM pets GROUP BY petType
fails.pred: SELECT pet_type, MAX(weight) AS max_weight FROM Pets GROUP BY pet_type

fails.q: List the maximum weight and type for each type of pet.
fails.gold: SELECT max(weight) ,  petType FROM pets GROUP BY petType
fails.pred: SELECT PetType, MAX(weight) AS max_weight FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType

fails.q: Find the first name of students who have cat or dog pet.
fails.gold: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat' OR T3.pettype  =  'dog'
fails.pred: SELECT s.Fname FROM Student s JOIN Has_Pet hp ON s.StuID = hp.StuID JOIN Pets p ON hp.PetID = p.PetID WHERE p.PetType IN ('cat','dog')

fails.q: What are the first names of every student who has a cat or dog as a pet?
fails.gold: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Fname FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat' OR T3.pettype  =  'dog'
fails.pred: SELECT S.Fname FROM Student S JOIN Has_Pet HP ON S.StuID = HP.StuID JOIN Pets P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID WHERE P.PetType IN ('cat','dog')

fails.q: What are the ids of the students who do not own cats as pets?
fails.gold: SELECT stuid FROM student EXCEPT SELECT T1.stuid FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat'
fails.pred: SELECT Student.StuID FROM Student LEFT JOIN Has_Pet ON Student.StuID = Has_Pet.StuID LEFT JOIN Pets ON Has_Pet.PetID = Pets.PetID WHERE Pets.PetType <> 'cat' OR Pets.PetType IS NULL

fails.q: What is the first name of every student who has a dog but does not have a cat?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.fname ,  T1.age FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'dog' AND T1.stuid NOT IN (SELECT T1.stuid FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid JOIN pets AS T3 ON T3.petid  =  T2.petid WHERE T3.pettype  =  'cat')
fails.pred:
SELECT S.Fname
FROM Student S
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM Has_Pet HP
    JOIN Pets P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID
    WHERE HP.StuID = S.StuID AND P.PetType = 'dog'
)
AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM Has_Pet HP
    JOIN Pets P ON HP.PetID = P.PetID
    WHERE HP.StuID = S.StuID AND P.PetType = 'cat'
)

fails.q: Find the average and maximum age for each type of pet.
fails.gold: SELECT avg(pet_age) ,  max(pet_age) ,  pettype FROM pets GROUP BY pettype
fails.pred: SELECT PetType, AVG(pet_age) AS avg_age, MAX(pet_age) AS max_age FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType

fails.q: What is the average and maximum age for each pet type?
fails.gold: SELECT avg(pet_age) ,  max(pet_age) ,  pettype FROM pets GROUP BY pettype
fails.pred: SELECT PetType, AVG(pet_age) AS avg_age, MAX(pet_age) AS max_age FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType

fails.q: Find the average weight for each pet type.
fails.gold: SELECT avg(weight) ,  pettype FROM pets GROUP BY pettype
fails.pred: SELECT PetType, AVG(weight) AS average_weight FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType

fails.q: What is the average weight for each type of pet?
fails.gold: SELECT avg(weight) ,  pettype FROM pets GROUP BY pettype
fails.pred: SELECT PetType, AVG(weight) AS average_weight FROM Pets GROUP BY PetType

fails.q: Find the first name and age of students who have a pet.
fails.gold: SELECT DISTINCT T1.fname ,  T1.age FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid
fails.pred: SELECT S.Fname, S.Age FROM Student S JOIN Has_Pet HP ON S.StuID = HP.StuID

fails.q: Find the number of pets for each student who has any pet and student id.
fails.gold: SELECT count(*) ,  T1.stuid FROM student AS T1 JOIN has_pet AS T2 ON T1.stuid  =  T2.stuid GROUP BY T1.stuid
fails.pred:
SELECT Has_Pet.StuID, COUNT(*) AS pet_count
FROM Has_Pet
GROUP BY Has_Pet.StuID
HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

fails.q: For students who have pets , how many pets does each student have ? list their ids instead of names .
fails.gold: select count(*) ,  t1.stuid from student as t1 join has_pet as t2 on t1.stuid  =  t2.stuid group by t1.stuid
fails.pred:
SELECT Has_Pet.StuID, COUNT(*) AS pet_count
FROM Has_Pet
GROUP BY Has_Pet.StuID

fails.q: How many models does each car maker produce? List maker full name, id and the number.
fails.gold: SELECT T1.FullName ,  T1.Id ,  count(*) FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id;
fails.pred:
SELECT cm.Id, cm.FullName, COUNT(ml.Model) AS model_count
FROM car_makers cm
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON ml.Maker = cm.Id
GROUP BY cm.Id, cm.FullName

fails.q: What is the full name of each car maker, along with its id and how many models it produces?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.FullName ,  T1.Id ,  count(*) FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker GROUP BY T1.Id;
fails.pred:
SELECT cm.Id, cm.FullName, COUNT(ml.Model) AS ModelCount
FROM car_makers cm
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON ml.Maker = cm.Id
GROUP BY cm.Id, cm.FullName

fails.q: Which model of the car has the minimum horsepower?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id ORDER BY T2.horsepower ASC LIMIT 1;
fails.pred: SELECT ml.Model FROM model_list ml JOIN cars_data cd ON cd.Id = ml.ModelId ORDER BY CAST(cd.Horsepower AS REAL) ASC LIMIT 1

fails.q: What is the model of the car with the smallest amount of horsepower?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.Model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id ORDER BY T2.horsepower ASC LIMIT 1;
fails.pred:
SELECT ml.Model
FROM model_list ml
JOIN car_names cn ON ml.Model = cn.Model
JOIN cars_data cd ON cn.MakeId = cd.Id
ORDER BY CAST(cd.Horsepower AS INTEGER) ASC
LIMIT 1

fails.q: Find the model of the car whose weight is below the average weight.
fails.gold: SELECT T1.model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id WHERE T2.Weight  <  (SELECT avg(Weight) FROM CARS_DATA)
fails.pred:
SELECT model_list.Model
FROM model_list
JOIN car_names ON model_list.Model = car_names.Model
JOIN cars_data ON car_names.MakeId = cars_data.Id
WHERE cars_data.Weight < (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM cars_data)

fails.q: What is the model for the car with a weight smaller than the average?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.model FROM CAR_NAMES AS T1 JOIN CARS_DATA AS T2 ON T1.MakeId  =  T2.Id WHERE T2.Weight  <  (SELECT avg(Weight) FROM CARS_DATA)
fails.pred:
SELECT model_list.Model
FROM cars_data
JOIN model_list ON cars_data.Id = model_list.ModelId
WHERE cars_data.Weight < (SELECT AVG(Weight) FROM cars_data)

fails.q: Find the name of the makers that produced some cars in the year of 1970?
fails.gold: SELECT DISTINCT T1.Maker FROM CAR_MAKERS AS T1 JOIN MODEL_LIST AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.Maker JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T3 ON T2.model  =  T3.model JOIN CARS_DATA AS T4 ON T3.MakeId  =  T4.id WHERE T4.year  =  '1970';
fails.pred: SELECT cm.Maker FROM car_makers cm JOIN cars_data cd ON cd.Id = cm.Id WHERE cd.Year = 1970

fails.q: Find the make and production time of the cars that were produced in the earliest year?
fails.gold: SELECT T2.Make ,  T1.Year FROM CARS_DATA AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.MakeId WHERE T1.Year  =  (SELECT min(YEAR) FROM CARS_DATA);
fails.pred:
SELECT car_makers.Maker, cars_data.Year
FROM cars_data
JOIN car_names ON cars_data.Id = car_names.MakeId
JOIN car_makers ON car_names.Make = car_makers.Maker
WHERE cars_data.Year = (SELECT MIN(Year) FROM cars_data)

fails.q: What is the maker of the carr produced in the earliest year and what year was it?
fails.gold: SELECT T2.Make ,  T1.Year FROM CARS_DATA AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.MakeId WHERE T1.Year  =  (SELECT min(YEAR) FROM CARS_DATA);
fails.pred:
SELECT cm.Maker, cd.Year 
FROM cars_data cd 
JOIN car_names cn ON cd.Id = cn.MakeId 
JOIN car_makers cm ON cn.Make = cm.Maker 
ORDER BY cd.Year ASC 
LIMIT 1

fails.q: What is the name of each continent and how many car makers are there in each one?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.Continent ,  count(*) FROM CONTINENTS AS T1 JOIN COUNTRIES AS T2 ON T1.ContId  =  T2.continent JOIN car_makers AS T3 ON T2.CountryId  =  T3.Country GROUP BY T1.Continent;
fails.pred:
SELECT continents.Continent, COUNT(car_makers.Id) AS maker_count
FROM continents
LEFT JOIN countries ON countries.Continent = continents.ContId
LEFT JOIN car_makers ON car_makers.Country = countries.CountryId
GROUP BY continents.Continent

fails.q: How many car models are produced by each maker ? Only list the count and the maker full name .
fails.gold: select count(*) ,  t2.fullname from model_list as t1 join car_makers as t2 on t1.maker  =  t2.id group by t2.id;
fails.pred:
SELECT cm.FullName, COUNT(m.Model) AS model_count
FROM car_makers cm
JOIN model_list m ON m.Maker = cm.Id
GROUP BY cm.FullName

fails.q: What is the number of car models that are produced by each maker and what is the id and full name of each maker?
fails.gold: SELECT Count(*) ,  T2.FullName ,  T2.id FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.Maker  =  T2.Id GROUP BY T2.id;
fails.pred:
SELECT cm.Id, cm.FullName, COUNT(DISTINCT ml.Model) AS ModelCount
FROM car_makers cm
LEFT JOIN model_list ml ON ml.Maker = cm.Id
GROUP BY cm.Id, cm.FullName

fails.q: What is the accelerate of the car make amc hornet sportabout (sw)?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.Accelerate FROM CARS_DATA AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.MakeId WHERE T2.Make  =  'amc hornet sportabout (sw)';
fails.pred:
SELECT cd.Accelerate
FROM cars_data cd
JOIN car_names cn ON cd.Id = cn.MakeId
JOIN car_makers cm ON cn.Make = cm.Maker
WHERE cm.Maker = 'amc' AND cn.Model = 'hornet sportabout (sw)'

fails.q: How much does the car accelerate that makes amc hornet sportabout (sw)?
fails.gold: SELECT T1.Accelerate FROM CARS_DATA AS T1 JOIN CAR_NAMES AS T2 ON T1.Id  =  T2.MakeId WHERE T2.Make  =  'amc hornet sportabout (sw)';
fails.pred:
SELECT c.Accelerate
FROM cars_data c
JOIN car_names n ON c.Id = n.MakeId
JOIN car_makers m ON n.Make = m.Maker
WHERE m.Maker = 'amc' AND n.Model = 'hornet sportabout (sw)'

fails.q: How many car makers are there in france?

fails.q: What is the count of the car models produced in the United States?
fails.gold: SELECT count(*) FROM MODEL_LIST AS T1 JOIN CAR_MAKERS AS T2 ON T1.Maker  =  T2.Id JOIN COUNTRIES AS T3 ON T2.Country  =  T3.CountryId WHERE T3.CountryName  =  'usa';
